rulings.Case Title: SEIU-USWW, AN UNINCORPORATED ASSOCIATION ET AL VS. PREFERRED BUILDING SERVICES, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ; Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 28, 2019, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF SEIU-USWW, AN UNINCORPORATED ASSOCIATION, JUAN MARTINEZ's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Motion For Summary Adjudication. Plaintiffs SEIU-USWW and Juan Martinez's ("SEIU" or "Plaintiffs") motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant Preferred Building Services, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Preferred's alternative motion for summary adjudication is granted and denied in part. Preferred's motion is granted as to issue one. Preferred's motion is denied as to issues two through four because Preferred failed to carry its initial burden of production. The Court's tentative rulings was sent to counsel by email. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES FAHEY VS. SELECT PORTFOLIO SERVICING, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response To Form Interrogatories, Set One
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, January 28, 2019, Line 1, Plaintiff James Fahey's Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Response To Form Interrogatories, Set One. Pro Tem Judge Julia Campins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court holds that Professional Career Colleges, Magna Institute v. Superior Court (1989) 207 Cal.App.3d 490, 494 controls. Although the Plaintiff timely met and conferred on the second issuance of the Form Interrogatories at issue, because they were exactly duplicative of the prior Form Interrogatories, he cannot now compel responses as requested here. The Court reminds Plaintiff that there are alternative methods of discovering the information he seeks, and so he is not prevented from gathering relevant information from Defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Julia@campinsbenhambaker.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: COLIN HEILBUT VS. EQUINOX FITNESS SC SAN FRANCISCO, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Unopposed Motion And Motion For Letters Rogatory; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Eric K. Iwasaki
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, January 28, 2019, Line 2, Defendant Kevin Nguyen's Notice Of Unopposed Motion And Motion For Letters Rogatory. Pro Tem Judge Julia Campins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: As Defendant Nguyen has presented sufficient information to find that the issuance of letters rogatory is necessary and convenient, and in the absence of opposition from any other party, the Court grants Defendant's Motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Julia@campinsbenhambaker.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SEVGI CURTIS ET AL VS. PG&E CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 28, 2019, 2018, Line 10, PLAINTIFF JANOS LIBOR's Motion To Amend Complaint. Plaintiff Janos Libor's motion for leave to amend the First Amended Complaint is granted. Under California's policies favoring liberality in amendment and resolution of all disputed matters between the parties in the same lawsuit, the amendment need not relate to claims or defenses originally pleaded. PG&E has not established that it would be prejudiced by the amendment, because discovery has not been completed, and the currently assigned September 3, 2019 trial date is not imminent. In any event, there is a strong likelihood that the litigation will be stayed as a result of PG&E's anticipated bankruptcy filing. Any discovery disputes or motions to continue the trial date may be addressed by the appropriate department. Finally, it is not a ground for denying the motion that causation cannot be established as a matter of law; causation ordinarily presents a factual question for the jury. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
